behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is the real git rename signal flowing through CLI parsing and categorization. A mock diff/status can miss real rename encodings and thresholds from git output, so real subprocess + real repo state is the minimum to catch this class of failure. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is only about git change classification, with no AI/ML decision and no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit a file, rename it with `git mv`, run the CLI as a subprocess against that repo, and assert report shows 1 rename, 0 add, 0 delete for that change set.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Uses real git + subprocess, matching System boundary.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real git behavior for binary diffs and real CLI/process robustness. In-process stubs often return simplified text and can hide crashes triggered by actual binary diff output/exit behavior. Rejected higher levels: no LLM/model output quality and no chained agentic steps in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, commit a binary file (e.g., random bytes), modify it, run the CLI subprocess, assert non-zero binary count appears and process exits successfully without traceback.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires real repo and subprocess interaction.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The key failure mode is handling real git responses/exit codes for an unborn HEAD in an actual repository. Mocking git calls can mask behavior differences seen in real subprocess execution. Rejected higher levels: behavior text contains no AI/ML dependency and no multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new real git repo with no commits, run the CLI subprocess, assert successful exit and an empty structured report (no categories populated) instead of an error.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Directly validates real CLI + git edge case.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness against real repository history and real git semantics across adds/deletes/renames/modifies; using mocks risks mirroring implementation assumptions and missing true discrepancies. Rejected higher levels: no model judgment and no agentic chain in the behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a real repo history with known numbers of added, deleted, renamed, and modified files across commits; run CLI subprocess and assert reported counts exactly match the known ground truth.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: End-to-end verification requires real git history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged behavior is tied to real git index/worktree semantics and command behavior; mocks commonly hide index edge cases. Minimum reliable boundary is real repo + real CLI invocation. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is about git state handling only, not AI/ML output or multi-agent workflows.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create equivalent file changes once as unstaged and once staged, run the CLI subprocess for each case, and assert the structured outputs are identical.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Compares outputs across real git index states.
